title: Abaca fibre — Manila hemp for strong, thin sheets
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923, public domain) — fibre types for high-strength papers
notes: Abaca (Musa textilis, the Manila hemp plant) produces a strong long-fibre pulp suited for thin, strong sheets. Used commercially in banknote paper and conservation-grade materials. For home papermakers: covers preparing dried abaca fibre, blending, and the different beating requirements compared to cotton linter. The resulting sheet is translucent when thin and exceptionally strong.
